featured.title: The Hidden Cost of Algorithmic Opacity in Financial Services
featured.summary: When automated models make high-stakes credit, pricing, and risk decisions without explainability structures in place, organisations don't just face regulatory scrutiny — they accumulate invisible governance debt that surfaces at the worst possible moment.

articles.title: EU AI Act: What It Means for Your Governance Framework
articles.summary: A breakdown of high-risk AI system classifications, conformity requirements, and the operational changes global firms must embed before enforcement deadlines.

articles.title: From Black Box to Glass Box: Explainability in Practice
articles.summary: Explainability is not a compliance checkbox — it is a structural design choice. We explore the practical techniques that make model decisions auditable without sacrificing performance.

articles.title: How a Global Bank Reduced Model Risk Exposure by 40%
articles.summary: An end-to-end account of our engagement with a Tier-1 financial institution: mapping algorithmic decision points, introducing materiality-based oversight, and building a board-ready reporting layer.

articles.title: The Decision Stack: Mapping Accountability in Automated Systems
articles.summary: When an autonomous system makes a consequential decision, who is accountable? We introduce the Decision Stack framework — a layered model for assigning ownership across technical and business teams.

articles.title: Regulatory Divergence in AI: Navigating US, EU, and APAC Frameworks
articles.summary: As jurisdictions develop competing AI regulatory regimes, multinational organisations face a compliance patchwork. We map the key divergences and propose a unified governance architecture that satisfies all three.

articles.title: Autonomous Trading Systems: A Governance Blueprint
articles.summary: A deep-dive into our governance engagement with a quantitative asset manager — embedding real-time oversight controls, kill-switch protocols, and audit trails into a live algorithmic trading infrastructure.

articles.title: Building an AI Ethics Committee That Actually Delivers
articles.summary: Most AI ethics committees are window dressing. We outline the mandate, membership criteria, decision rights, and escalation pathways that make these bodies genuinely effective — and credible to regulators.

articles.title: Model Drift and the Governance Gap: A Quantitative Perspective
articles.summary: Edge-case drift is the leading cause of AI system failure in production. We quantify the governance gap between model deployment and ongoing oversight, and propose a monitoring architecture firms can implement today.
